title: 65 By ANT
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 65 participants undergoing calibrated laser pain stimulation with concurrent pain ratings. Participants received 30 laser stimuli at individually-determined pain-inducing intensities and provided verbal pain intensity ratings on a 0-10 scale. The study investigates the neural correlates of pain perception and subjective pain rating behavior through synchronized electrophysiological and behavioral measurements.
methods_description: Participants underwent a calibration phase to identify laser stimulation intensity corresponding to a pain rating of 7, followed by 30 experimental trials of laser stimulation at the calibrated intensity. After each stimulus, participants provided verbal pain ratings on a scale from 0 (no sensation) to 10 (unbearable pain). Participants were instructed to maintain fixation on a crosshair display with eyes open. Intertrial intervals were randomized at approximately 5 seconds following pain rating responses. Laser markers are delayed by 100ms relative to stimulus delivery.
